title: Liam Botham

{{short description|English rugby league & union footballer, and cricketer}} {{Use dmy dates|date=October 2021}} {{Use British English|date=October 2021}} {{infobox rugby league biography | name = Liam Botham | first = RU | image = | image_size = | caption = | alt = | fullname = Liam James Botham | birth_date = {{Birth date and age|df=yes|1977|8|26}} | birth_place = [[Doncaster]], South Yorkshire, England | death_date = | death_place = | height = {{convert|6|ft|0|in|m|abbr=on}} | weight = {{convert|15|st|0|lb|kg|abbr=on}} | ru_position = [[Wing (rugby union)|Wing]] | ru_club1 = [[West Hartlepool R.F.C.|West Hartlepool]] | ru_year1start = 1996 | ru_year1end = 1997 | ru_appearances1 = 14 | ru_points1 = 15 | ru_club2 = [[Cardiff RFC]] | ru_year2start = 1997 | ru_year2end = 2000 | ru_appearances2 = 29 | ru_points2 = 120 | ru_club3 = [[Newcastle Falcons]] | ru_year3start = 2000 | ru_year3end = 2003 | ru_appearances3 = 74 | ru_points3 = 111 | ru_club4 = [[Leeds Tykes]] | ru_year4start = 2003 | ru_year4end = 2004 | ru_appearances4 = 19 | ru_points4 = 10 | ru_teamA = [[England national under-20 rugby union team|England U21]] | ru_yearAstart = 1997 | ru_yearAend = 1998 | position = {{rlp|SR}} | club1 = [[Leeds Rhinos]] | year1start = 2003 | year1end = 05 | appearances1 = 15 | tries1 = 4 | goals1 = 0 | fieldgoals1 = 0 | points1 = 16 | club2 = {{nowrap|→ [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]]}} | year2start = 2004{{small|(loan)}} | appearances2 = 8 | tries2 = 3 | goals2 = 7 | fieldgoals2 = 0 | points2 = 26 | club3 = [[Wigan Warriors]] | year3start = 2005 | appearances3 = 5 | tries3 = 0 | goals3 = 0 | fieldgoals3 = 0 | points3 = 0 | module = {{Infobox cricketer | child = yes | batting = Right-handed | bowling = Right-arm [[medium-fast]] | role = | family = [[Ian Botham]] (father)
[[James Botham]] (son) | club1 = [[Hampshire County Cricket Club|Hampshire]] | year1 = 1996 | columns = 2 | column1 = [[First-class cricket|FC]] | matches1 = 3 | runs1 = 31 | bat avg1 = 10.33 | 100s/50s1 = 0/0 | top score1 = 30 | deliveries1 = 330 | wickets1 = 8 | bowl avg1 = 33.50 | fivefor1 = 1 | tenfor1 = 0 | best bowling1 = 5/67 | catches/stumpings1 = 2/– | column2 = [[List A cricket|LA]] | matches2 = 1 | runs2 = 1 | bat avg2 = 1.00 | 100s/50s2 = 0/0 | top score2 = 1 | deliveries2 = 24 | wickets2 = 0 | bowl avg2 = – | fivefor2 = – | tenfor2 = – | best bowling2 = – | catches/stumpings2 = 0/– | date = 7 July 2021 | source = https://www.espncricinfo.com/player/liam-botham-9165 ESPNcricinfo }} }} '''Liam James Botham''' (born 26 August 1977) is an English former professional sportsman who played both codes of [[rugby football]] and [[cricket]]. He is the son of former [[England cricket team|England]] cricketer [[Ian Botham]]. ==Career== ===Cricket=== Botham was born in [[Doncaster]] and initially followed in his father's footsteps, playing county cricket for [[Hampshire County Cricket Club|Hampshire]]. He played three matches in the 1996 season.{{cite web|url=https://cricketarchive.com/Players/4/4213/4213.html|title=Liam Botham|url-access=subscription|publisher=[[CricketArchive]]|access-date=7 July 2021}} He took the wicket of former England captain [[Mike Gatting]] on his County Championship debut. After retiring from rugby, there were signs that Botham might return to playing competitive cricket; in 2006, he played in an England XI victory against Lancashire in a testimonial match for [[Andrew Flintoff]] at [[Old Trafford Cricket Ground|Old Trafford]]. ===Rugby union=== Botham left cricket in 1997 after one senior season and signed for [[rugby union]] club [[West Hartlepool R.F.C.|West Hartlepool]], where he played as a wing or fullback. He later moved to [[Cardiff RFC|Cardiff]] and [[Newcastle Falcons]]. While at Newcastle the club won the 2001 [[Tetley Bitter Cup]], in the final of which he was a replacement,{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_union/1187949.stm|title=Newcastle snatch Cup glory|publisher=BBC|date=24 February 2001|access-date=26 December 2009}} and reached the final of the [[European Shield]]. Botham made his début for the England under-21 team in 1997 against France, and then played in the SANZAR tournament in South Africa in 1998.{{Cite web |date=1998-11-22 |title=Rugby Union: Botham in swing on the wing |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-union-botham-in-swing-on-the-wing-1186648.html |access-date=2022-11-09 |website=The Independent |language=en}} He was called up to the senior [[England national rugby union team|England]] squad by [[Clive Woodward]] for their [[2000 England rugby union tour of South Africa|2000 tour of South Africa]].{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/sport/rugby_union/760455.stm|title=Johnson reclaims England captaincy|work=BBC Sport|publisher=[[British Broadcasting Corporation]]|date=7 June 2000|access-date=9 February 2016}} He played in tour matches against the Leopards and Griqualand West{{Cite web|title=BBC News {{!}} RUGBY UNION {{!}} England tame Leopards|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/sport/rugby_union/789693.stm|access-date=2021-06-04|website=news.bbc.co.uk}}{{Cite web|title=BBC News {{!}} RUGBY UNION {{!}} Rampant England smash Griquas|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/1/hi/sport/rugby_union/799310.stm|access-date=2021-06-04|website=news.bbc.co.uk}} but never won a senior international cap. ===Rugby league=== After becoming disillusioned with rugby union, he switched sports again in 2003 to rugby league. He initially joined the [[Bradford Bulls]] and had a short spell playing for their academy team. He then signed a dual-code contract with [[Super League]] club [[Leeds Rhinos]], who felt that signing the inexperienced Botham was less of a risk as he also played for their partner rugby union club [[Leeds Tykes]]. In 2004, Botham also played for [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]] on loan, and in 2005 signed for [[Wigan Warriors]], feeling he could not get a regular place in the Rhinos team. In his three seasons in rugby league, usually as a {{rlp|sr}}, he played 28 senior games, scoring 7 tries and 6 goals.[http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/liam-botham/summary.html Rugby League Project] In 2005, Botham announced his retirement aged 27 after a [[neck]] injury. ==Post-playing career== Since retiring, Botham has run a sports travel company.{{cite news |title=Liam Botham avoids driving ban after legal mix-up |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/cricket/liam-botham-avoids-driving-ban-after-legal-mix-up-1782427 |access-date=21 June 2024 |work=Yorkshire Post |date=18 February 2017}} ==Personal life== Botham married Sarah-Jayne, with whom he has three children. Their son [[James Botham|James]] plays rugby union for [[Cardiff Blues]] and made his full international debut for [[Wales national rugby union team|Wales]] in 2020. The couple later divorced and Botham married Lisa Harrison in 2013. She gave birth to their son Benji in July 2015.[http://www.belfasttelegraph.co.uk/entertainment/news/lisa-harrison-and-liam-botham-bowled-over-by-birth-of-baby-son-31400296.html Lisa Harrison and Liam Botham bowled over by birth of baby son], [[Belfast Telegraph]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== * {{ESPNcricinfo|id=9165}} * {{ESPNscrum|126713}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Botham, Liam}} [[Category:1977 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:20th-century English sportsmen]] [[Category:21st-century English sportsmen]] [[Category:Bradford Bulls players]] [[Category:Cricketers from Doncaster]] [[Category:Cumberland cricketers]] [[Category:English cricketers of 1969 to 2000]] [[Category:English cricketers]] [[Category:English rugby league players]] [[Category:English rugby union players]] [[Category:Hampshire cricketers]] [[Category:Leeds Rhinos players]] [[Category:Leeds Tykes players]] [[Category:London Broncos players]] [[Category:Newcastle Red Bulls players]] [[Category:People educated at Rossall School]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Doncaster]] [[Category:Rugby union players from Doncaster]] [[Category:Sons of life peers]] [[Category:Wigan Warriors players]]
